current_mirror.design_intent: edge-case-oriented (multi-season aggregation, single-season, undrafted, multi-team), deliberately NOT target-population-oriented

fixture_overlap_check.note: Even Option A would require regenerating the mirror to get overlap; the regeneration effort is symmetric across options.

promoted_weekly_outcomes_export_check.finding: 6 rows across 2025 weeks 1-3 for two players, sourced from offline_fixture -- scaffold coverage only; NOT viable as a real 2025 outcome population today
promoted_weekly_outcomes_export_check.implication: the candidate player_season_coverage_v0 artifact is currently the only real, source-backed 2025 outcome data committed anywhere in this chain

decision_1_target_population.question: What target/outcome population should the first controlled run use?

option_a_fixture_n38.acceptable_residual_use: none required -- run MECHANICS are already proven by the #105/#106 dry-run matrix, so a fixture-population rehearsal run would add no new information
reasons: governance_status is fixture; outcome values are hand-seeded, not source-backed
n=38 is too small for the per-position breakdowns the #102 design requires
zero overlap with the current mirror means a mirror regeneration is needed anyway
a result on a fixture population cannot ground any empirical player-history claim

option_b_real_mounted_tiber_data_2025_po.outcome_layer_source: the candidate player_season_coverage_v0 artifact's 610 REG 2025 rows (season_ppr as the target outcome; identity/position plus row-level provenance fields source_refs and identity_confidence -- required so the target-population gate can verify source-backing per row; no input-feature payloads)
outcome_layer_caveats: the artifact is candidate_evidence_artifact_not_promoted; using its 2025 rows as an EXPERIMENT outcome layer does not promote it and must be stated in every derived report
2025 rows may ONLY supply the outcome column and identity -- never any input feature (existing #104 structural guards already enforce the input side)

the promoted weekly-outcomes export was checked and is a 6-row week-1 offline fixture; if TIBER-Data later promotes a full real 2025 outcome export, it becomes the PREFERRED outcome source and the target-population gate must re-run against it
option_b_real_mounted_tiber_data_2025_po.feasibility_evidence: 610-player population; 485 (79.5%) joinable to real 2022-2024 history; every position has >= 66 joinable players

prerequisite_path: build a real target-population OUTCOME mirror (2025 REG rows; outcome, identity, and row-level provenance fields source_refs/identity_confidence; no input-feature payloads) from the pinned artifact via a committed deterministic generator script
regenerate the INPUT mirror as a generated (not hand-picked) subset: all 2022-2024 REG rows for the target population's players (1,145 rows), trimmed to scaffold-needed fields
keep outcome mirror and input mirror as two separate files so the input side structurally cannot carry target-season rows
re-run the #99/#100 gate and the #105 dry-run matrix against the new mirrors before any run authorization

decision_2_mirror_coverage.question: What mirror coverage is required before a run?
requirements: do NOT vendor the full 8.8MB Data artifact into Forecast

mirrors must be GENERATED by a committed, deterministic, network-free script that reads the sha256-pinned source artifact -- never hand-picked rows (the #104 4-player mirror was hand-selected for edge cases, which was correct for #103 and is wrong for a run)

the input mirror must include ALL 2022-2024 REG rows for EVERY player in the chosen target population (estimated 1,145 rows, ~2.2MB trimmed); a player absent from the source for a season is a documented absence, not a mirror gap
partial coverage of the target population is acceptable ONLY as honest absence (player has no source rows), never as sampling
players with target outcomes but no player-history rows (125 today, e.g. rookies) STAY in the matrix as feature-less rows with nulls preserved -- they are the no-history subgroup, not exclusions
player-history rows without target outcomes remain excluded with the existing reason (player_history_features_without_target_row)

each mirror ships with a provenance companion: source repo/path, sha256 pin, refs, row counts, per-position counts, target-window scope, trimming rationale, exclusion reasons, and an explicit no-2025-rows-as-inputs statement for the input mirror
re-run or extend the #99/#100 player-season coverage gate whenever the mirror/source scope changes materially (including any upstream artifact regeneration that changes the sha256)

decision_3_minimum_overlap.question: What minimum overlap is needed before a controlled run is authorized?

thresholds.shuffled_control_feasibility: every included position group must have >= 2 feature-bearing rows so a derangement exists (subsumed by the per-position minimum)

decision_3_minimum_overlap.calibration_note: Thresholds are conservative floors chosen against observed feasibility (485 joinable, >= 66 per position, 79.5% share), NOT tuned to the current fixture and NOT tuned post hoc. They exist to make per-position breakdowns and the position-stratified shuffled control meaningful. Lowering any threshold requires explicit review in the run-authorizing issue before any metric is computed.

decision_4_required_gates.question: What gate or provenance checks are required before a run?
required: re-verify the #99/#100 player-season coverage gate against the current pinned artifact sha256; re-run the gate if the sha changes

NEW target-population gate: outcome rows are source-backed (source_refs present, identity_confidence checked), season/season_type/position scope correct, row grain correct, candidate status acknowledged with an explicit not-promoted statement, and outcome values are real (not fixture-seeded)

NEW mirror-overlap gate: machine-checkable evaluation of the regenerated dry-run matrix report against the decision-3 thresholds, fail-closed, with a decision ceiling of may_authorize_run_issue (never may_run) -- consistent with the #100 gate-ceiling pattern
verify no 2025 player-history summaries are consumed as 2025 inputs (structural: the input mirror contains no 2025 rows; #104 guards enforce it in code)
verify target outcomes are never copied into feature rows (the dry-run matrix already omits outcome values by construction; keep its test)
verify null semantics remain intact (existing #104/#106 tests plus the dry-run missingness report)

decision_5_next_issue.proposed_title: Forecast: build real target-population mirror for player-history run
proposed_scope: committed deterministic generator script producing the 2025 outcome mirror (outcome, identity, and row-level provenance fields; no input-feature payloads) and the regenerated 2022-2024 input mirror (all rows for the target population, trimmed) from the sha256-pinned artifact
re-run the #99/#100 gate against the pins
re-run the #105 dry-run matrix against the new mirrors and regenerate its report (expected: joined rows on the order of 485)
implement the mirror-overlap gate evaluating that report against the decision-3 thresholds (ceiling: may_authorize_run_issue)
explicitly_not_in_next_issue: no run, no Run 3, no training/tuning/evaluation/comparison, no metric computation, no baseline change, no production feature binding, no TIBER-Data/Teamstate change, no artifact promotion
decision_5_next_issue.after_that: a separate issue -- Forecast: authorize controlled player-history run after population gate -- may authorize the three-arm run ONLY once the overlap gate passes

non_goals_confirmed: no full-artifact vendoring in this PR
no 2025 summaries consumed as 2025 input features
